observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the:

End-user units are to prepare the Requisition and Issue (RIS) every month based on the logbooks being maintained, and submit the same to the General Services Officer (GSO)-designate.



b. GSO - Designate to:
i. Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS submitted and forward the said report to the municipal accountant as her basis for recognizing supplies expenses.

ii.	Spearhead the conduct of a semestral (or oftener, depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory, report the results thereof in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and submit the report to the Audit Team; and

iii.	Determine the actual number of days delayed for the goods that were only delivered after the lapse of the delivery period as indicated in the contract and submit the same to the municipal accountant for the computation of liquidated damages.

c. Municipal Accountant to:
i. Recognize expense in the general journal based on the said SSMI and not by subtracting the balance of the inventory as reflected in the GL from the total amount of inventory as reflected in the inventory report of the end-user unit; and

ii. Compute the liquidated damages to be imposed on the supplier due to the delay in the delivery of the goods.


d. Bids and Awards Committee should consider entering into a framework agreement with the supplier, if necessary, for the succeeding procurement of goods to avoid an internal agreement with the supplier that is not governed by the terms and conditions of the contract.

We also recommended that the LCE: 
assign other personnel as the inspection officer, who conducts the inspection and verification of the items if they conform with the specifications, to observe proper segregation of duties, and 


issue a demand letter requiring the supplier to deliver the goods not yet delivered and payment of the corresponding liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Engineer to assess the project implementation viability of two projects identified to have right of way issues, and if warranted, recommend the cessation of said projects and propose technically feasible alternative projects for re-appropriation, including the reallocation of funds associated with any duplicated project; and
Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the current manpower complement of the MEO, and if found insufficient, consider the hiring of additional personnel to support the conduct of detailed engineering activities and facilitate the timely implementation of projects.

We recommended the LCE direct the BAC to use competitive bidding in the procurement of meals and snacks and consider entering into a Framework Agreement with the winning bidder to streamline and simplify the procurement process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer, in coordination with the MENRO, to provide training for garbage collectors and other personnel directly involved in solid waste collection, in compliance with Section 23 (b) of  RA No. 9003, and utilize the  TESDA Garbage Collector NC 1 training program to equip them with the necessary skills on the proper handling of waste and minimizing their exposure to potential health risks and injuries.
During the exit conference, Management signified compliance with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GFPS to:
facilitate the regular updating and maintenance of the GAD Database by utilizing sex-disaggregated data from the CBMS and other relevant sources;
institutionalize a more rigorous evaluation process for proposed GAD PPAs by requiring the proper use of gender analysis tools such as the HGDG and ensuring alignment with documented gender issues, as required under Sections C.1.4 and C.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. Proposals that do not address gender gaps should be revised or excluded from the GAD Plan; and
strengthen the functionality of the GFPS by providing adequate support and capacity-building.
During the exit conference, Management said that the GAD Database will be updated in July 2025 using the results of CBMS. They also committed to send the GFPS to GAD-related trainings.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LSB to:
submit properly dated SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 quarterly reports not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter and annual reports not later than the 15th day of February of the following year to the DepEd Central Office, Sangguniang Bayan of Sudipen, La Union, and the Regional Offices of DBM and DILG;
ensure that reports are posted within the prescribed period and sufficiently documented to guarantee timely and accurate reporting; and
assure that updated guidelines and issuances related to the allocation and utilization of SEF be duly distributed to the concerned officers and employees.

We recommended that the LCE direct the AAO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to formulate internal policies and procedures on the evaluation and approval of salary loan applications of employees, which includes provision of certification of NTHP in the evaluation and approval of loans; and submit to the LCE for her approval. Thereafter, implement the approved policies and procedures and encourage employees to adhere thereon.
The Accounting Office has started maintaining logbook/record for the certification of net pay issued to employees applying for loans and strictly not issue certification if net take home pay is already below the mandated minimum amount of ₱5,000.00. To further ensure compliance with the audit recommendation, payroll will no longer have below ₱5,000.00 net, and payroll and disbursement of RATA will be separated from the regular payroll starting April 2025.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit all obsolete AFs to the Audit Team for inspection and destruction pursuant to Section 99 of GAAM Volume 1.

observations.recommendation:

Any error, omission or misstatement appearing in the books of accounts shall be the joint responsibility of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant.
We recommended that in order to maximize the benefits of the iTAX system provided by the Provincial Government and to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of the List of Delinquent Real Property Taxpayers, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor coordinate with the Provincial Government of La Union to check the installed iTAX system in the municipality.
We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to instruct her staff to update posting in the RPTAR to have a reliable basis in the preparation of the List of Delinquent Real Property Taxpayers.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the management inform in writing the former accountable officers to turnover the OR booklet in their possession, or file legal sanctions.

We recommended that inorder to have an assurance that the Building Code of the Philippines is properly enforced within the Municipality and inorder to increase collection from building permit fees, the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Barangay Officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay and to coordinate with the Building Official when they notice that there are structures being built within their barangay.
We recommended that the Revenue Code of the Municipality reviewed and corrected in order to align with the provisions to relevant laws, rules and regulations.  Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee and Sanggunian Bayan Members, must see to it that proposed and approved tax ordinance or revenue measure are consistent and aligned with the relevant laws, rules and regulations.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the Treasury Office to keep files of photocopied required documents and to properly fill-up the form for application of business permits.

We recommended that the BAC:
inform all the units/department that PPMP will be based on every quarter requirement and not on every semester;
plan and prepare the APP with advance procurement actions in order to ensure that the goods are available prior or on the first day of the quarter; and
prepare the APP in accordance with the format approved by the GPPB which shall contain schedule of each procurement activities.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to comply with the requirements as provided in the above cited provisions of laws, rules and regulations particularly on the procurement of medicines.
The audit team recommended for the Municipal Accountant to withhold retention money from all payment of infrastructure projects and release only upon final acceptance of the works or upon submission by the contractor of a substitute in the form of either irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money, provided that the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken.
We recommended for the municipal government to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will answer structural defects/failures that may be discovered from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2 to ensure that the contractor will perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 (a) of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that all Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed to suppliers and contractors in all procurement of goods and infrastructures be posted in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed period to provide timely information to the public.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that after the budget proposal has been approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and/or other oversight bodies, the procuring entity’s budget office shall furnish a copy of the procuring entity’s budget proposal as well as the corresponding PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for its review and consolidation into the proposed APP.  The APP should include not only common use supplies but all the municipality’s programs/projects/activities.  Likewise, the correct format should be used as prescribed by the GPPB.
The audit team recommended that a signboard or billboard as required by the above Circulars be posted for all PPAs of the municipality with the essential features/information to ensure transparency and encourage public participation to the activities of the municipality. Further, we recommended for the agency to provide a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and to notify the audit team on the posting of the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices for the purpose of validation and verification.

We recommended that management ensure and monitor the full implementation of the PPAs appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and economic services.
Thereafter, we recommended that management provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each procurement activities in the APP.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to implement the Municipality’s funded pre-disaster activities and projects in accordance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, issued by the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG to reduce the impact of hazards and calamities and to require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate its implementation.
Further, we have recommended that the management stop the practice of procuring and paying transactions which are not included in the appropriation ordinance or LDRRMFIP and to require the refund of P57,605.00 from the concerned officials and employees who signified the consummation of the above transaction.

We recommended that the municipality continue to appropriate at least 5% of their total annual budget for gender and development plans and programs to ensure the promotion of public welfare and basic services addressing gender issues.
We recommended that management continue to identify programs and projects that address gender issues and concerns and promote gender equality and women empowerment.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the GAD Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents. Further, for the GFPS to ensure and monitor the effective implementation of the annual GAD PPAs.

We recommended that management properly plan their procurement activities and ensure that all programs and projects funded from the municipality’s Share from the Collection of Tobacco Excise Taxes  under R.A. No. 7171 are effectively and efficiently implemented in order for the farmers to receive the optimum benefits from the projects that could improve their living conditions.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on the payment of BAC honoraria specifically on Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.4 and DBM Circular No. 2004-5A in order to maximize the utilization of the funds appropriated for particular project or programs. Moreover, for the Municipal Budget Officer to declare only savings from projects or programs which have been entirely undertaken or completed.
Further, we recommended that the BAC and TWG Chairmen and Members refund the excess payment of P178,756.03.

We recommended that the municipality must reflect in the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents the actual cost of the rice seeds for transparency.  Thereafter, collection from farmers must be properly receipted and recorded for accountability.

We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to ensure that Daily Time Record certified correct by the employee concerned and verified by his immediate supervisor, approved Applications for Leave and approved Travel Orders, if any, Minutes of Regular Session for elective officials and all necessary documents are attached to the municipal payrolls both for permanent and job order employees in order to ascertain the validity and legality of the claims.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities.  We further reiterated that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG and for the Municipal Accountant to complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

Further, to issue demand letter upon determination of the details of the above receivable.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to account and document the unused balances of the funds released by the National Government; and determine if they are no longer needed so that the said amount be reverted in accordance to Paragraph 3 of EO 431.

Otherwise, we request management to suspend the salary of the Accountant/person responsible for failure to submit the required reports without justifiable reason, until compliance with the requirements.

We recommended that Management ensure that funds allocated for Special Education Fund be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG, series of 1998, 2000 and 2001 to effectively attain the purpose for which the fund was created. We recommended further that the above expenditures be appropriated to the General Fund.

We further recommended for the submission of the lacking documents as specified in Annex E. Also, a certification shall be attached that the expenses itemized in the budget of SEF were not included in the DepEd budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the Local Finance Committee and the LDRRMO Designate to strictly comply with the provisions of DILG, NEDA, DBM and DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 2007 and NDRRMC, DBM AND DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 1 dated March 25, 2013, to enhance transparency and accountability in the use of LDRRMF.

We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation for the management to formulate a plan designed to address gender issues and concerns and to strictly comply with guidelines prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We have recommended that after the budget proposal has been approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and/or other oversight bodies, the procuring entity’s budget office shall furnish a copy of the procuring entity’s budget proposal as well as the corresponding PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for its review and consolidation into the proposed APP.  The APP should include not only common use supplies but all the municipality’s programs/projects/activities.  Likewise, the correct format should be used as prescribed by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR | For the management to include all PPAs in their APP in the year 2017
